ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Online data collection tools like Google Forms often export multiple-response questions with data concatenated in cells. The concat.split (cSplit) family of functions splits such data into separate  cells. The package also includes functions to stack groups of columns and  to reshape wide data, even when the data are "unbalanced"---something  which reshape (from base R) does not handle, and which melt and dcast from  reshape2 do not easily handle.

Online data collection tools like Google Forms often export multiple-response questions with data concatenated in cells. The concat.split (cSplit) family of functions splits such data into separate  cells. The package also includes functions to stack groups of columns and  to reshape wide data, even when the data are "unbalanced"---something  which reshape (from base R) does not handle, and which melt and dcast from  reshape2 do not easily handle.

name: r-splitstackshape
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = "1.4.8.1" %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-splitstackshape
  version: {{ version|replace("-", "_") }}

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/splitstackshape_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/splitstackshape/splitstackshape_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: e5755c8fc413c1de632107046e0830606229c97c3ad1b68f49bb8957a127f3e8

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-data.table >=1.9.4
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-data.table >=1.9.4

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('splitstackshape')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('splitstackshape')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: http://github.com/mrdwab/splitstackshape
  license: GPL-3
  summary: Online data collection tools like Google Forms often export multiple-response questions with data concatenated in cells. The concat.split (cSplit) family of functions splits such data into separate  cells. The package also includes functions to stack groups of columns and  to reshape wide data, even when the
    data are "unbalanced"---something  which reshape (from base R) does not handle, and which melt and dcast from  reshape2 do not easily handle.
  license_family: GPL3
  license_file: '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/GPL-3'

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: splitstackshape
# Type: Package
# Title: Stack and Reshape Datasets After Splitting Concatenated Values
# Version: 1.4.6
# Date: 2018-07-22
# Author: Ananda Mahto
# Maintainer: Ananda Mahto <mrdwab@gmail.com>
# Description: Online data collection tools like Google Forms often export multiple-response questions with data concatenated in cells. The concat.split (cSplit) family of functions splits such data into separate  cells. The package also includes functions to stack groups of columns and  to reshape wide data, even when the data are "unbalanced"---something  which reshape (from base R) does not handle, and which melt and dcast from  reshape2 do not easily handle.
# License: GPL-3
# LazyData: TRUE
# LazyLoad: yes
# Depends: R (>= 2.10)
# Imports: data.table (>= 1.9.4)
# URL: http://github.com/mrdwab/splitstackshape
